definition:
1. the hollow bottom  or stand of a cup , Lat. fundus, (Iliad by Homer), etc. 
2. of the sea, the bottom, depth , (Hesiod), etc. 
3. the bottom  or foundation  of a thing, in plural, χθόνα ἐκ πυθμένων κραδαίνειν (Aeschulus Tragicus); ἐκ π. ἔκλινε κλῇθρα (Sophocles Tragicus); δίκας π. the anvil-stand  on which is forged the sword of retribution, (Sophocles Tragicus) 
4. the bottom, stock, root of a tree , (Odyssey by Homer):—;metaphorically the stem  or stock  of a family, (Aeschulus Tragicus); σμικροῦ γένοιτ᾽ ἂν σπέρματος π. μέγας, i. e. great things might come from small, (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
 (ML)
